description: Trusting a size or index from an ioctl argument without bounds-checking, leading to buffer overflow or out-of-bounds access in the kernel.
body: Failure mode: an ioctl handler reads a length or index from the user-supplied argument and uses it to size a copy or index an array without validating it against the real bounds; a crafted value overflows a kernel buffer or reads/writes out of bounds. Detection signals: KASAN reports out-of-bounds on an ioctl path; the size/index comes straight from the user arg into a memcpy/array access; fuzzing the ioctl crashes the kernel. Do not trust user-provided sizes or indices. Safer fallback: validate every length and index against the allocated size and array bounds before use, reject out-of-range values with -EINVAL, and fuzz the ioctl interface to confirm no out-of-bounds access.
